item.title: 如何将页脚放在页面底部或较低的内容。 | How place footer at bottom of page or content which is lower.
item.description:
首先创建一个包含所有元素或组件的容器。 然后创建页眉、主要内容和页脚。

 HTML <div class="container"> <div class="header">This is navbar/header</div> <div class="main-content"></div> <div class="footer">This is footer</div> </div>

向主容器添加一些样式，因为它将包含我们所有的数据，所以我们使用 flex 和 flex direction 列。 我们还给出了 min-height: 100vh 以便容器将具有视口的完整高度。

 .container{ width: 100%; min-height: 100vh; display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; flex-direction: column; }

现在，我们将添加一些样式到我们必须给底部的页脚：0; 这样它就会粘在容器的底部，我们还必须提供 margin-top : auto; 所以它会尽可能地将页脚推到底部。

 .header{ width: 100%; background-color: bisque; height: 8vh; text-align: center; } .main-content{ display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; align-items: center; background-color: azure; } .footer{ bottom: 0; margin-top: auto; text-align: center; background-color: rgb(154, 178, 178); width: 100%; height: 8vh; }


原文:

First create a container which contains all the elements or components. Then create header, main-content, and footer.

HTML
<div class="container">
         <div class="header">This is navbar/header</div>
         <div class="main-content"></div>
         <div class="footer">This is footer</div>
      </div>

Add some styling to the main container as it will contain all our data, so we are using flex and flex direction column. We also giving min-height: 100vh so that the container will have the full height of the viewport.

.container{
    width: 100%;
    min-height: 100vh;
    display: flex;
    justify-content: center;
    align-items: center;
    flex-direction: column;
}

Now, we will add some styling to the footer we have to give bottom:0; so that it will stick to the bottom of container and we also have to give margin-top : auto; so it will push the footer to bottom as mush as possible.

.header{
    width: 100%;
    background-color: bisque;
    height: 8vh;
    text-align: center;
}
.main-content{
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    justify-content: center;
    align-items: center;
    background-color: azure;
}
.footer{
    bottom: 0;
    margin-top: auto;
    text-align: center;
    background-color: rgb(154, 178, 178);
    width: 100%;
    height: 8vh;
}
item.author: himanshu_99

item.title: 如何成为一名优秀的开发者？ | How to become a good developer?
item.description:
大家好，今天我将与大家分享一些关于如何成为一名优秀开发人员的技巧。 所以在拖延之前让我们开始吧。

要成为一名优秀的开发人员，我们需要遵循一些步骤，它们是：

  • 源码阅读习惯
  • 实践
  • 遵循结构
  • 沟通
  • 文档
  • 社区
  • 想要学习

源码阅读习惯

一句名言说，如果你想成为一个好的演讲者，你必须先听； 不成为一个好的倾听者，你就不能成为一个好的演讲者。 如果你想写出好的代码，你也应该阅读其他开发者的代码。 通过阅读其他开发人员的代码，您将了解他们的逻辑并了解他们解决相同问题的观点。

然而，大多数开发人员不喜欢阅读源代码，因为他们觉得它很无聊。 但是阅读其他开发人员的代码让我们有机会批评他们在编写代码时所犯的错误。 通过识别其他软件开发人员在其代码中所犯的错误，您将能够避免它们。

阅读源代码是成为优秀开发人员的第一步，这一点很重要。

你的习惯将决定你的未来

实践

小时候学骑自行车，摔倒了，受了点小伤，但还是想骑。 有一天，你终于可以骑自行车了。

同样，编程工作也需要同样的方法。 通过编写代码和练习，您将成为一名优秀的程序员。 成为一名优秀的开发人员需要练习。

实践让你完美

遵循结构

在大多数情况下，系统会要求您向现有应用程序添加一些新功能。

如果你得到这种工作，我建议你不要先写代码。 相反，花一些时间来理解编码结构。 发现应用程序的行为及其遵循的结构。 一旦你理解了这些东西，你就可以开始编写代码了。

继续现有路径

沟通

当我加入第一家公司时，我的沟通能力很差，但我擅长掌握新技能。 由于缺乏沟通技巧，我没有机会成为团队负责人。

在发生 COVID-19 之后，沟通技巧非常重要，因为管理和沟通实际上需要良好的沟通技巧。

如果你有良好的发展技能而没有沟通技巧，你就无法成长更多。 在您的团队中则相反，如果某人具有良好的沟通能力，但缺乏足够的发展技能，那么他/她可能会有成长的机会。 有效的沟通将帮助您实现目标。

小心你的话一旦说出来就无法收回

文档

为您从事的每个项目制作个人文档。 记下有关该项目的所有要点。 每当您忘记某些内容时，您都可以轻松访问文档。

每当我为项目制作文档时，我都会存储指向外部资源、登录凭据等的链接。

文档不是关于我们做什么，而是我们搜索什么

社区

每当您遇到编码问题时，请在 Google 上搜索解决方案。 如果您没有得到正确答案，请将您的问题发布在 StackOverflow 或其他社区平台上。

当您陷入困境时，社区支持至关重要。 为社区做贡献也很重要。

一个人，我们能做的很少； 在一起，我们可以完成这么多

想要学习

传统上，我们从书本上学习，但现在，我们从互联网上学习。 然而，发展是一个我们相互学习的领域。 有些开发人员在询问他们的疑问时感到害羞，并且不感谢他人的帮助，因此下次他们询问时他们没有得到回应。

因此，如果您不确定某事，只需询问您的同事即可。 不要害怕提问。

永远不要停止学习，因为生活永远不会停止教学

结论

作为开发人员，我总是尝试遵循上述原则。 如果您是新手，我相信遵循这些原则将帮助您成为一名优秀的开发人员。

喜欢，评论和分享。
来自萨法拉吉的爱。



原文:

Hi guys, today I am going to share with you some tips on how to become a good developer. So before going to make any delay let’s start.

To become a good developer, we need to follow some steps, which are:

  • The habit of source code reading
  • Practice
  • Follow the structure
  • Communication
  • Documentation
  • Community
  • Eager to learn

The habit of source code reading

A famous quote says that if you want to become a good speaker, you have to listen first; You can’t become a good speaker without becoming a good listener. If you want to write good code, you should read other developers' code as well. By reading the other developer's code, you will understand their logic and see their point of view on solving the same problem.

However, most developers don't like to read source code because they find it boring. But reading other developers' code gives us the opportunity to criticize the mistakes they made when writing that code. By identifying the mistakes other software developers have made in their code, you will be able to avoid them.

It is important to read source code as a first step toward becoming a good developer.

Your habits will determine your future

Practice

When you were a child, Learning to ride a bicycle, you fell down, and you got a little injured, but you still wanted to ride it. One day came and you finally could ride the bicycle.

Similarly, programming work requires the same approach. You will become a good programmer by writing code and practicing. Being a good developer requires practice.

Practice makes you perfect

Follow the structure

In most cases, you will be asked to add some new features to the existing application.

If you get this kind of work, I recommend you don't write the code first. Rather, take some time to understand the coding structure. Discover the application's behavior and the structure it follows. Once you understand these things, you can start writing code.

Continue on the existing path

Communication

My communication skills were poor when I joined my first company, but I was good at acquiring new skills. Due to my lack of communication skills, I didn't get the opportunity to become a team leader.

After the COVID-19 situation, communication skill is very important because managing and communicating virtually requires good communication skills.

You cannot grow more if you have good developing skills and no communication skills. The opposite is true in your team, if someone has good communication skills, but not enough skills in development, then there might be opportunities for him/her to grow. Effective communication will help you to achieve your goals.

Be careful with your words once spoken they cannot be taken back

Documentation

Make personal documentation for every project you work on. Note down all the important points about the project. Whenever you forget something, you can easily access the documentation.

Whenever I make documentation for a project, I store links to external resources, login credentials, etc.

Documentation isn't about what we do, but what we search for

Community

Whenever you are stuck on a coding problem, search on Google for a solution. If you didn't get the correct answer, post your question on StackOverflow or another community platform.

When you are stuck, community support is crucial. Contributing to the community is also important.

Alone, we can do so little; together, we can accomplish so much

Eager to learn

Traditionally, we learn from books, but nowadays, we learn from the internet. However, development is such a field where we learn a lot from one another. There are developers who feel shy in asking about their doubts and are not grateful for others' help, so the next time they ask they do not receive a response.

Therefore, if you are not sure of something, simply ask your colleagues. Don't be afraid to ask questions.

Never stop learning because life never stops teaching

Conclusion

I always try to follow the above principle as a developer. I am sure that following these principles will help you to become a good developer if you are a fresher.

Like, comment and share.
Love from Sarfaraj.
item.author: yourmdsarfaraj

item.title: 使用 React、TypeScript 和 Storybook 创建组件设计系统 | Creating a component design system using React, TypeScript and Storybook
item.description:
在处理项目时，创建设计系统非常有用。 如果您是设计师，那么您已经熟悉这个概念。 拥有一个包含品牌颜色、字体大小和内容格式等的设计文件......在开发方面同样适用，因为我们需要确保前端与设计文件匹配。

对于使用过 Tailwindcss 或 Bootstrap 等 CSS 框架的人来说，使用设计系统的想法并不新鲜，因为这些框架都遵循自己的设计系统。 这很棒，因为我们可以单独查看组件，它使我们能够为所有组件创建文档和测试。

例如，您可以向客户展示 Storybook 中的设计系统，他们可以看到所有组件的外观。 甚至可以更改一些值，例如背景颜色和字体大小，以便他们可以看到不同的版本。 对于设计师来说，他们将能够看到他们的设计在代码中的样子，如果有任何动画，他们也可以看到。

设置项目

让我们从使用 Storybook 设置样板 React 项目开始。 导航到一个目录并打开您的命令行工具。 将下面的代码复制并粘贴到命令行中以设置您的项目。

 npx create-react-app my-app --template typescript cd my-app npx sb init

您现在应该有两个运行脚本。 运行以下两个脚本来启动服务器。

 # Starts the React Application npm run start
 # Starts the Storybook component design system npm run storybook

创建故事书组件

如果您访问在 Web 浏览器中打开的 Storybook 开发页面，您应该会看到一些示例组件，以便您了解它是如何工作的。 现在让我们创建一个 UI 组件，以便您了解使用 Storybook 创建组件的感觉。

首先创建一个名为Hero的文件夹并将其放在 src 中的 stories 文件夹中。 现在创建 3 个文件并将它们放在Hero文件夹中。 创建文件Hero.css 、 Hero.stories.tsx和Hero.tsx 。

将下面的代码复制并粘贴到相应的文件中。

src/stories/Hero/Hero.css

 @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Quicksand:wght@400;500;700&display=swap') ; .hero { background : rgb ( 236 , 236 , 236 ); border : 1rem solid rgb ( 220 , 220 , 220 ); max-width : 50rem ; width : 100% ; border-radius : 1rem ; } .hero-content { width : 100% ; display : flex ; flex-flow : column nowrap ; } .hero-content h1 { font-family : 'Quicksand' , sans-serif ; color : #000000 ; text-transform : uppercase ; text-align : center ; } .hero-content img { max-width : 50rem ; width : 100% ; } .hero-content p { font-family : 'Quicksand' , sans-serif ; color : #000000 ; padding : 0.5rem ; } #preloader { width : 50rem ; height : 50rem ; } #loader { display : block ; position : relative ; left : 50% ; top : 50% ; width : 150px ; height : 150px ; margin : -75px 0 0 -75px ; border-radius : 50% ; border : 3px solid transparent ; border-top-color : #9370db ; -webkit-animation : spin 2s linear infinite ; animation : spin 2s linear infinite ; } #loader :before { content : '' ; position : absolute ; top : 5px ; left : 5px ; right : 5px ; bottom : 5px ; border-radius : 50% ; border : 3px solid transparent ; border-top-color : #ba55d3 ; -webkit-animation : spin 3s linear infinite ; animation : spin 3s linear infinite ; } #loader :after { content : '' ; position : absolute ; top : 15px ; left : 15px ; right : 15px ; bottom : 15px ; border-radius : 50% ; border : 3px solid transparent ; border-top-color : #ff00ff ; -webkit-animation : spin 1.5s linear infinite ; animation : spin 1.5s linear infinite ; } @-webkit-keyframes spin { 0 % { -webkit-transform : rotate ( 0deg ); -ms-transform : rotate ( 0deg ); transform : rotate ( 0deg ); } 100 % { -webkit-transform : rotate ( 360deg ); -ms-transform : rotate ( 360deg ); transform : rotate ( 360deg ); } } @keyframes spin { 0 % { -webkit-transform : rotate ( 0deg ); -ms-transform : rotate ( 0deg ); transform : rotate ( 0deg ); } 100 % { -webkit-transform : rotate ( 360deg ); -ms-transform : rotate ( 360deg ); transform : rotate ( 360deg ); } }

src/stories/Hero/Hero.stories.tsx

 import { ComponentStory , ComponentMeta } from ' @storybook/react ' ; import { Hero } from ' ./Hero ' ; export default { title : ' Hero/Hero ' , component : Hero , } as ComponentMeta < typeof Hero > ; const Template : ComponentStory < typeof Hero > = ( args ) => < Hero {... args } /> ; export const Primary = Template . bind ({}); Primary . args = { id : 1 , title : ' Mountain ' , img : ' https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1464278533981-50106e6176b1?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=2274&q=80 ' , content : ' Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Duis mollis tellus et libero feugiat, vitae dignissim augue pulvinar. ' , };

src/stories/Hero/Hero.tsx

 import ' ./Hero.css ' ; interface Heroprops { id : number ; title : string ; img : string ; content : string ; loading : boolean ; } export const Hero = ({ id , title , img , content , loading , ... props }: Heroprops ) => { return ( <> < div className = " hero " > { loading ? ( < div id = " preloader " > < div id = " loader " >< /div > < /div > ) : ( < div className = " hero-content " > < h1 > { title } < /h1 > < img src = { img } alt = { title } / > < p > { content } < /p > < /div > )} < /div > < / > ); }; export default Hero ;

App.css

将文件中的所有代码替换为以下代码。

 *, * ::before , * ::after { padding : 0 ; margin : 0 ; box-sizing : border-box ; } html { font-size : 16px ; } body { font-size : 1rem ; font-family : 'Quicksand' , sans-serif ; color : #000000 ; background-color : #222 ; } .container { margin : 0 auto ; width : 100% ; max-width : 50rem ; }

App.tsx

将文件中的所有代码替换为以下代码。

 import Hero from ' ../src/stories/Hero/Hero ' ; import ' ./App.css ' ; const App = () => { return ( <> < div className = " container " > < Hero id = { 1 } title = { ' Mountain ' } img = { ' https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1464278533981-50106e6176b1?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=2274&q=80 ' } content = { ' Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Duis mollis tellus et libero feugiat, vitae dignissim augue pulvinar. ' } // Change this value to true to see the loading animation loading = { false } / > < /div > < / > ); }; export default App ;

为您的 React 应用程序和 Storybook 服务器重新加载服务器，您现在应该会看到一个带有图像的 Hero 组件。 如果由于某种原因图像损坏，您可以将图像 url 更改为其他内容。 在App.tsx和Hero.stories.tsx文件中执行此操作。

您可以更改 id、title、img、content 甚至加载状态。

最后的想法

这就是使用 Storybook 的简要介绍。 要了解更多信息，请访问Storybook 主网站。



原文:

Creating a design system is extremely useful when working on projects. If you are a designer then you are already familiar with the concept. Having a design file that has the brand colours, font sizes and content formatting etc... In terms of development the same applies because we need to ensure that the frontend matches the design file.

For those of you who have used a CSS framework like Tailwindcss or Bootstrap, the idea of working with a design system is not new because these frameworks adhere to their own design systems. Storybook essentially gives developers a way to create their own component design system on the frontend. This is great because we can view the components in isolation and it gives us the ability to create documentation and testing for all of the components.

So for example you could show a client the design system in Storybook and they could see how all of the components look. It's even possible to change some of the values like background colours and font sizes so they can see different versions. In the case of a designer they will be able to see what their designs look like in code and if there are any animations they can see that too.

Setting up the project

Let's start by setting up a boilerplate React project with Storybook. Navigate to a directory and open your command line tool. Copy and paste the code below into the command line to setup your project.

npx create-react-app my-app --template typescript
cd my-app
npx sb init

You should now have two run scripts. Run both of the scripts below to start the servers.

# Starts the React Application
npm run start

# Starts the Storybook component design system
npm run storybook

Creating Storybook components

If you go to the Storybook development page that opened in your web browser you should be presented with some example components so you can get a feel for how it works. Now let's create a UI component so that you can see what it's like to create components using Storybook.

Firstly create a folder called Hero and put it inside of the stories folder which is inside of src. Now create 3 files and put them inside of the Hero folder. Create the files Hero.css , Hero.stories.tsx and Hero.tsx.

Copy and paste the code below into their corresponding files.

src/stories/Hero/Hero.css

@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Quicksand:wght@400;500;700&display=swap'); .hero { background: rgb(236, 236, 236); border: 1rem solid rgb(220, 220, 220); max-width: 50rem; width: 100%; border-radius: 1rem;
} .hero-content { width: 100%; display: flex; flex-flow: column nowrap;
} .hero-content h1 { font-family: 'Quicksand', sans-serif; color: #000000; text-transform: uppercase; text-align: center;
} .hero-content img { max-width: 50rem; width: 100%;
} .hero-content p { font-family: 'Quicksand', sans-serif; color: #000000; padding: 0.5rem;
} #preloader { width: 50rem; height: 50rem;
} #loader { display: block; position: relative; left: 50%; top: 50%; width: 150px; height: 150px; margin: -75px 0 0 -75px; border-radius: 50%; border: 3px solid transparent; border-top-color: #9370db; -webkit-animation: spin 2s linear infinite; animation: spin 2s linear infinite;
} #loader:before { content: ''; position: absolute; top: 5px; left: 5px; right: 5px; bottom: 5px; border-radius: 50%; border: 3px solid transparent; border-top-color: #ba55d3; -webkit-animation: spin 3s linear infinite; animation: spin 3s linear infinite;
} #loader:after { content: ''; position: absolute; top: 15px; left: 15px; right: 15px; bottom: 15px; border-radius: 50%; border: 3px solid transparent; border-top-color: #ff00ff; -webkit-animation: spin 1.5s linear infinite; animation: spin 1.5s linear infinite;
} @-webkit-keyframes spin { 0% { -webkit-transform: rotate(0deg); -ms-transform: rotate(0deg); transform: rotate(0deg); } 100% { -webkit-transform: rotate(360deg); -ms-transform: rotate(360deg); transform: rotate(360deg); }
} @keyframes spin { 0% { -webkit-transform: rotate(0deg); -ms-transform: rotate(0deg); transform: rotate(0deg); } 100% { -webkit-transform: rotate(360deg); -ms-transform: rotate(360deg); transform: rotate(360deg); }
}

src/stories/Hero/Hero.stories.tsx

import { ComponentStory, ComponentMeta } from '@storybook/react'; import { Hero } from './Hero'; export default { title: 'Hero/Hero', component: Hero,
} as ComponentMeta<typeof Hero>; const Template: ComponentStory<typeof Hero> = (args) => <Hero {...args} />;

export const Primary = Template.bind({}); Primary.args = { id: 1, title: 'Mountain', img: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1464278533981-50106e6176b1?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=2274&q=80', content: 'Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Duis mollis tellus et libero feugiat, vitae dignissim augue pulvinar.',
};

src/stories/Hero/Hero.tsx

import './Hero.css'; interface Heroprops { id: number; title: string; img: string; content: string; loading: boolean;
} export const Hero = ({ id, title, img, content, loading, ...props }: Heroprops) => { return ( <> <div className="hero"> {loading ? ( <div id="preloader"> <div id="loader"></div>
 </div>
 ) : ( <div className="hero-content"> <h1>{title}</h1>
 <img src={img} alt={title} />
 <p>{content}</p>
 </div>
 )} </div>
 </>
 );
}; export default Hero;

App.css

Replace all of the code inside of the file with the code below.

*,
*::before,
*::after { padding: 0; margin: 0; box-sizing: border-box;
} html { font-size: 16px;
} body { font-size: 1rem; font-family: 'Quicksand', sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: #222;
} .container { margin: 0 auto; width: 100%; max-width: 50rem;
}

App.tsx

Replace all of the code inside of the file with the code below.

import Hero from '../src/stories/Hero/Hero'; import './App.css'; const App = () => { return ( <> <div className="container"> <Hero id={1} title={'Mountain'} img={ 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1464278533981-50106e6176b1?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=2274&q=80' } content={ 'Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Duis mollis tellus et libero feugiat, vitae dignissim augue pulvinar.' } // Change this value to true to see the loading animation loading={false} />
 </div>
 </>
 );
}; export default App;

Reload the server for your React app and the Storybook server and you should now see a Hero component with an image. If for whatever reason the image is broken you can just change the image url to something else. Do this in the App.tsx and Hero.stories.tsx files.

The Hero component has some customisable options in Storybook. You can change the id, title, img, content and even the loading state.

Final Thoughts

And thats a brief introduction to using Storybook. To learn more visit the main Storybook website.
item.author: andrewbaisden

item.title: 适配器模式？ | The Adapter Pattern?
item.description:
所以最近我遇到了一个挑战，我必须创建一种方法，允许用户在 web 应用程序以及使用浏览器 API 的 android 上授予相机/麦克风的权限，而 android 是一个 cordova 插件。 在 android 中具有相同的概念有点不同。 所以首先需要检查浏览器的权限是否允许，如果不允许则请求权限。 逻辑是山姆。 因此，我不必重复自己，因为我必须创建（如果我不使用适配器模式两个不同的提供程序，加上在我想使用适配器的文件上使用重复代码）我发现了适配器模式。 这个概念是一种非常有趣的工程模式，第一次有点难以理解，但你会看到它有多么有益，而且下次你做起来会容易得多。

我会给你一个最近项目的例子，希望能帮助你在工作中使用它。 我还使用mic-check包进行麦克风检查，因为我在某处看到它并认为它很酷。 下面的例子还没有完全完成，但你会明白的......

 type AdapterType = { requestCameraPermission : () => void , requestMicrophonePermission : () => void , hasCameraPermission : () => Promise < boolean > , hasMicrophonePermission : () => Promise < boolean > , }; export class PermissionsApiAdapter { hasCameraPermission = () => navigator . permissions . query ({ name : ' camera ' }). then (( permissionObj ) => { console . log ( permissionObj . state ); if ( permissionObj . state !== ' granted ' ) { return true ; } return false ; }); hasMicrophonePermission = () => navigator . permissions . query ({ name : ' microphone ' }) . then (( permissionObj ) => { console . log ( permissionObj . state ); if ( permissionObj . state !== ' granted ' ) { return true ; } return false ; }); requestCameraPermission = () => { const cameraPermissions = navigator . mediaDevices && navigator . mediaDevices . getUserMedia ({ video : true , }); if ( cameraPermissions ) { cameraPermissions . then (() => { alert ( ' Accept camera permissions ' ); console . log ( ' request camera permissions are enabled ' ); return Promise . resolve ( cameraPermissions ); }) . catch (( err ) => Promise . reject ( new Error ( ` ${ err . name } : ${ err . message } ` )) ); } }; requestMicrophonePermission = () => { const audioPermissions = navigator . mediaDevices && navigator . mediaDevices . getUserMedia ({ audio : true , }); if ( audioPermissions ) { audioPermissions . then (() => { alert ( ' Accept permissions microphone ' ); console . log ( ' request microphone permissions enabled ' ); return Promise . resolve ( audioPermissions ); }) . catch (( err : MediaPermissionsError ) => { const { type } = err ; if ( type === MediaPermissionsErrorType . SystemPermissionDenied ) { console . log ( ' browser does not have permission to access camera or microphone ' ); } else if ( type === MediaPermissionsErrorType . UserPermissionDenied ) { console . log ( ' user didnt allow app to access camera or microphone ' ); } else if ( type === MediaPermissionsErrorType . CouldNotStartVideoSource ) { console . log ( ' camera is in use by another application (Zoom, Skype) or browser tab (Google Meet, Messenger Video ' ); } else { Promise . reject ( new Error ( ` ${ err . name } : ${ err . message } ` )); } }); } }; } class WebAppsPermission { constructor ( permissionAdapter : AdapterType ) { this . permissionAdapter = permissionAdapter ; } permissionAdapter : AdapterType ; requestCameraPermission = () => this . permissionAdapter . requestCameraPermission (); requestMicrophonePermission = () => this . permissionAdapter . requestMicrophonePermission (); hasCameraPermission = () => this . permissionAdapter . hasCameraPermission (); hasMicrophonePermission = () => this . permissionAdapter . hasMicrophonePermission (); } export default WebAppsPermission ;

现在对于 Android 应用程序，适配器具有相同的逻辑，但功能略有不同。

 type AndroidAdapterType = { requestCameraPermission : () => void , requestMicrophonePermission : () => void , hasCameraPermission : () => Promise < boolean > , hasMicrophonePermission : () => Promise < boolean > , }; export class AndroidPermissionsApiAdapter { hasCameraPermission = () => window . cordova . plugins . diagnostic . isCameraAuthorized ( ( authorized ) => { console . log ( `App is ${ authorized ? ' authorized ' : ' denied ' } access to the camera` ); }, ( error ) => { console . error ( `The following error occurred: ${ error } ` ); }, false ); hasMicrophonePermission = () => window . cordova . plugins . diagnostic . isMicrophoneAuthorized ( ( authorized ) => { console . log ( `App is ${ authorized ? ' authorized ' : ' denied ' } access to the microphone` ); }, ( error ) => { console . error ( `The following error occurred: ${ error } ` ); } ); requestCameraPermission = () => { window . cordova . plugins . diagnostic . requestCameraAuthorization ({ successCallback ( status ) { console . log ( `Authorization request for camera use was ${ status === window . cordova . plugins . diagnostic . permissionStatus . GRANTED ? ' granted ' : ' denied ' } ` ); }, errorCallback ( error ) { console . error ( error ); }, externalStorage : false , }); }; requestMicrophonePermission = () => { window . cordova . plugins . diagnostic . requestMicrophoneAuthorization ( ( status ) => { if ( status === window . cordova . plugins . diagnostic . permissionStatus . GRANTED ) { console . log ( ' Microphone use is authorized ' ); } }, ( error ) => { console . error ( error ); } ); }; } class AndroidPermissions { constructor ( permissionAdapter : CordovaAdapterType ) { this . permissionAdapter = permissionAdapter ; } permissionAdapter : AndroidAdapterType ; requestCameraPermission = () => this . permissionAdapter . requestCameraPermission (); requestMicrophonePermission = () => this . permissionAdapter . requestMicrophonePermission (); hasCameraPermission = () => this . permissionAdapter . hasCameraPermission (); hasMicrophonePermission = () => this . permissionAdapter . hasMicrophonePermission (); } export default AndroidPermissions ;

当我想在特定文件夹中使用它时，我的情况是在Context Provider中

const adapter = new PermissionsApiAdapter (); const cordovaAdapter = new AndroidPermissionsApiAdapter (); const mediaPermission = new WebAppsPermission ( window . cordova ? cordovaAdapter : adapter );

所以现在我可以在我的 Provider 上使用适配器，或者在我想要的代码的任何地方。

希望这是有道理的☺️

阿纳布查特吉 - May 12 5月12日

hritik chaudhary - hritik chaudhary - May 12 5月12日



原文:

So recently I came across with a challenge where I had to create a way of allowing users to grant permission for camera/ mic in a web app and also on an android using the browser API and for the android is was a cordova plugin. In android is a little bit different with the same concept. So firstly need to check if the permission from the browser is allowed if not then to request permission. The logic is the sam. So instead of repeating myself, as I had to create (if I wouldn't use the adapter pattern two different Providers as well, plus use repetitive code on the files I want to use the adapter) I found out about the adapter pattern. That concept is one of the engineering patterns which is quite interesting, a bit difficult to get your head around the first time but then you can see how beneficial it can be plus next time you do it is much easier.

I will give you an example on a recent project and hope that helps you to use it on your work. I also use the mic-check package for microphone check, cause I saw it somewhere and thought it was cool. The example below is not fully finished but you will get the idea...

type AdapterType = { requestCameraPermission: () => void, requestMicrophonePermission: () => void, hasCameraPermission: () => Promise<boolean>, hasMicrophonePermission: () => Promise<boolean>,
}; export class PermissionsApiAdapter { hasCameraPermission = () => navigator.permissions.query({ name: 'camera' }).then((permissionObj) => { console.log(permissionObj.state); if (permissionObj.state !== 'granted') { return true; } return false; }); hasMicrophonePermission = () => navigator.permissions .query({ name: 'microphone' }) .then((permissionObj) => { console.log(permissionObj.state); if (permissionObj.state !== 'granted') { return true; } return false; }); requestCameraPermission = () => { const cameraPermissions = navigator.mediaDevices && navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: true, }); if (cameraPermissions) { cameraPermissions .then(() => { alert('Accept camera permissions'); console.log('request camera permissions are enabled'); return Promise.resolve(cameraPermissions); }) .catch((err) => Promise.reject(new Error(`${err.name} : ${err.message}`)) ); } }; requestMicrophonePermission = () => { const audioPermissions = navigator.mediaDevices && navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true, }); if (audioPermissions) { audioPermissions .then(() => { alert('Accept permissions microphone'); console.log('request microphone permissions enabled'); return Promise.resolve(audioPermissions); }) .catch((err: MediaPermissionsError) => { const { type } = err; if (type === MediaPermissionsErrorType.SystemPermissionDenied) { console.log( 'browser does not have permission to access camera or microphone' ); } else if (type === MediaPermissionsErrorType.UserPermissionDenied) { console.log('user didnt allow app to access camera or microphone'); } else if ( type === MediaPermissionsErrorType.CouldNotStartVideoSource ) { console.log( 'camera is in use by another application (Zoom, Skype) or browser tab (Google Meet, Messenger Video' ); } else { Promise.reject(new Error(`${err.name} : ${err.message}`)); } }); } };
} class WebAppsPermission { constructor(permissionAdapter: AdapterType) { this.permissionAdapter = permissionAdapter; } permissionAdapter: AdapterType; requestCameraPermission = () => this.permissionAdapter.requestCameraPermission(); requestMicrophonePermission = () => this.permissionAdapter.requestMicrophonePermission(); hasCameraPermission = () => this.permissionAdapter.hasCameraPermission(); hasMicrophonePermission = () => this.permissionAdapter.hasMicrophonePermission();
} export default WebAppsPermission;

Now for the Android Apps the adapter has same logic with a bit different functionality.


type AndroidAdapterType = { requestCameraPermission: () => void, requestMicrophonePermission: () => void, hasCameraPermission: () => Promise<boolean>, hasMicrophonePermission: () => Promise<boolean>,
}; export class AndroidPermissionsApiAdapter { hasCameraPermission = () => window.cordova.plugins.diagnostic.isCameraAuthorized( (authorized) => { console.log( `App is ${authorized ? 'authorized' : 'denied'} access to the camera` ); }, (error) => { console.error(`The following error occurred: ${error}`); }, false ); hasMicrophonePermission = () => window.cordova.plugins.diagnostic.isMicrophoneAuthorized( (authorized) => { console.log( `App is ${ authorized ? 'authorized' : 'denied' } access to the microphone` ); }, (error) => { console.error(`The following error occurred: ${error}`); } ); requestCameraPermission = () => { window.cordova.plugins.diagnostic.requestCameraAuthorization({ successCallback(status) { console.log( `Authorization request for camera use was ${ status === window.cordova.plugins.diagnostic.permissionStatus.GRANTED ? 'granted' : 'denied' }` ); }, errorCallback(error) { console.error(error); }, externalStorage: false, }); }; requestMicrophonePermission = () => { window.cordova.plugins.diagnostic.requestMicrophoneAuthorization( (status) => { if ( status === window.cordova.plugins.diagnostic.permissionStatus.GRANTED ) { console.log('Microphone use is authorized'); } }, (error) => { console.error(error); } ); };
} class AndroidPermissions { constructor(permissionAdapter: CordovaAdapterType) { this.permissionAdapter = permissionAdapter; } permissionAdapter: AndroidAdapterType; requestCameraPermission = () => this.permissionAdapter.requestCameraPermission(); requestMicrophonePermission = () => this.permissionAdapter.requestMicrophonePermission(); hasCameraPermission = () => this.permissionAdapter.hasCameraPermission(); hasMicrophonePermission = () => this.permissionAdapter.hasMicrophonePermission();
} export default AndroidPermissions;

And when I wanted to use it in a specific folder in my case was inside a Context Provider

const adapter = new PermissionsApiAdapter(); const cordovaAdapter = new AndroidPermissionsApiAdapter(); const mediaPermission = new WebAppsPermission( window.cordova ? cordovaAdapter : adapter );

So now I can use the adaptors on my Provider, or at any place of my code I want to.

Hope that makes sense ☺️

Arnab Chatterjee - May 12

hritik chaudhary - May 12
item.author: vikirobles

item.title: 有效的数字字谜 | Valid Numeric Anagram
item.description:
给定 2 个正整数，编写一个函数来确定它们是否具有相同数量的重复/非重复元素。

想法：

  • 由于这个问题使用数字而不是字符串，因此可以使用一些 JS 技巧来获取单个数字
    • 我们可以先字符串化并获取每个位置的字符
    • 或者我们可以使用数学公式得到相同的结果
  • 数学方法采用与基数排序相同的技巧
  • 使用相同的数学策略来练习，因为它是更难的解决方案
  • 总督算法工程 - Apr 24 4月24日



原文:

1 Multi-pointers Question 2 Frequency Counter Question

Given 2 postive integers, write a function to determine if they have the same number of repeated / non-repeated elements.

Thoughts:

  • Since this question uses numbers instead of string it is possible to employ some JS tricks to get the individual digits
    • we could stringify first and get the character in each position
    • OR we could use mathematical formula to get the same result
  • The mathematical method employs the same trick as in Radix Sort
  • Employ the same mathematical tactic to practice since it is the more difficult solution
  • Stringify method is simpler but is hard to translate over to other languages as a solution 
function getNumOfDigits(num) { if (num === 0) return 1; return Math.floor(Math.log10(num)) + 1;
}
function getDigitByPos(num, pos) { if (num === 0) return 0; return Math.floor(num / Math.pow(10, pos)) % 10;
} function ValidNumericAnagram(numX, numY) { if (getNumOfDigits(numX) !== getNumOfDigits(numY)) return false; let digitCounter = {}; let limit = getNumOfDigits(numX); for (let i=0; i<limit; i++) { const digit = getDigitByPos(numX, i); if (digitCounter[digit] === undefined) { digitCounter[digit] = 0 } digitCounter[digit]++; } for (let i=0; i<limit; i++) { const digit = getDigitByPos(numY, i); if (digitCounter[digit] === undefined) { return false; } digitCounter[digit]--; } return true;
}
1 Multi-pointers Question 2 Frequency Counter Question

Doge Algo Engineering - Apr 24
item.author: henryong92

item.title: 2022 年 7 大增强现实见解和趋势 | Top 7 Augmented Reality Insights and Trends for 2022
item.description:
该帖子最初发表于Banuba 博客。

与许多其他技术一样，AR 已从科幻梦想转变为噱头，进而成为例行公事。 然而，它还远未达到顶峰，它获得了新用户，扩大了使用范围，并与其他尖端技术产生了协同作用。

在这里，我们将讨论 2022 年 7 个最显着的增强现实趋势，展示 5 个强调其重要性的有趣统计数据，并描述 AR 如何扩展到新行业——制造、电子学习和医疗保健。

为什么新的 AR 技术很重要：5 个统计数据

增强现实的重要性可以从以下数字中看出。

  • 智能手机和平板电脑仍然是最流行的与之交互的工具，但工人在工厂和工厂中为此使用智能眼镜。
  • 根据 MarketingDive 的数据，15% 的零售连锁店和商店使用某种形式的 AR。 但是，它们包括耐克和宜家等世界知名品牌。 此外，还有 32% 的人计划将 AR 添加到他们的商店或已经在实施。
  • 对 AR 初创公司的投资也在增加。 根据 Crunchbase 的数据，去年它达到了 19 亿美元。 投资者希望看到虚拟世界的影响越来越大，AR 在新的垂直领域的扩张，以及更多的大品牌采用它。
  • 增强现实也有质的增长。 品牌不断加大对AR 广告的投资。 到 2023 年初，全球 AR 广告收入可能超过 20 亿美元。

这就是今年增强现实世界将发生的变化。

到年底，这个数字将增加到14亿。

这是由多种因素共同造成的。

首先，AR 变得更容易获得。 一方面，越来越多的人拥有能够支持它的设备。 另一方面，技术本身正在变得更加精简和优化。 例如，Banuba Face AR SDK 甚至可以在 iPhone 5S 上运行并覆盖 80% 的 Android 设备。

其次，企业用户和政府机构开始意识到 AR 提供的机会。 微软与美国军方 签订合同，宝马为维修专家推出AR眼镜等。

元宇宙登上舞台

发明这个词的科幻作家尼尔·斯蒂芬森（Neal Stephenson）对他的想法目前的实施方式并没有留下深刻的印象。

然而，投资者看到了这项技术的潜力。 目前，有几个备受瞩目的虚拟世界正在开发中：Decentraland、Meta Platforms、Nvidia Omniverse 和 Ali Metaverse 等等。 他们想成为数十万人的中心，涌入的资金表明他们不会很快消失。

例如，摩根大通在Metaverse 中开设了第一家银行分行时就引起了轰动，称其为“万亿美元的机会”。 两年后，元界产业价值将达到 800 亿美元左右。

美国和欧洲的公司正在迎头赶上。 这包括像 Meta 这样的大牌和较小的初创公司，用户热切地使用新的购物体验。

例如，在 Instagram 上， 44% 的用户已经尝试过。 他们发现这比在 TikTok 上的现场购物更方便。

到 2023 年，直播购物部分将仅在美国市场销售价值 25B 美元的商品。 到 2026 年，它将占据整个电子商务市场的 20% 。

影响直播购物受欢迎程度的三个主要因素。

它既方便又具有实体购物的个人对个人部分拥有许多社交媒体订阅者的公司可以迅速将他们变成买家

让有影响力的人推广产品更加顺畅

每个人都克隆 TikTok

其他网络尝试延长视频或添加新功能来竞争。 还有一些直接复制 TikTok 。

短视频市场的所有参与者都在推动AR技术获得优势。 例如，TikTok 和 Snapchat 让每个用户都可以创建 AR 效果。 虽然这家中国公司最近才这样做，但用户创建的镜头已被浏览超过 3.5 万亿次。

这种趋势在全球范围内都能感受到，比如印度社交网络Chingari定期添加更多“印度化”的 AR 口罩，最近还实施了直播购物。

实体店将包含 AR 元素

一旦 COVID-19 限制开始消失，人们就开始返回实体店——他们就像这个过程一样。 然而，精通技术的零售商倾向于将物理和数字（“phygital”）方法相结合，以获得两全其美的效果。 他们开发虚拟试穿应用程序，让人们测试产品并显示在哪里购买。 他们还可以安装智能镜子和 AR 照相亭，让购物更有趣，更值得社交媒体使用。

这种方法对青少年和年轻人的影响最大，因为三分之一的 Z 世代更喜欢 AR 驱动的商店。

在大多数情况下，实体店老板都会从 AR 试穿开始，因为它易于理解并带来切实的优势——更多的销售额和更少的退货。 但还有其他选择。

耐克向人们介绍他们的供应链，并展示服装和鞋子的特点。

宝马允许客户在 AR 中更改汽车的内部元素以查看其外观，并提供虚拟试驾服务。

制造商将 AR 添加到他们的工具箱中

更严重的是，制造公司已开始为员工配备 AR 眼镜以提高效率。 它们有四个最常见的用例：

  • 员工入职和技能提升。 通过在实际材料和仪器上逐步展示流程，公司可以帮助员工学习新技术。
  • 物品识别。 人工智能可以比人类更快地从几十个零件中找到特定零件。
  • 远程工作。 专家可以通过 AR 覆盖分享他们的知识，从而避免前往偏远地区。
  • 细胞设计。 工程师可以将 AR 对象放置在物理位置，以快速设计制造单元。

仍然有一些问题阻碍了 AR 在工业上的广泛采用，但这一趋势绝大多数都对它有利——正如达索、西门子、劳斯莱斯和其他主要品牌所证明的那样。

由于低硬件要求和低维护成本，Web AR 是用户和企业最容易实现的增强现实。

它的质量和性能不如其他选项好，但在许多情况下这是一个有效的折衷方案。 示例包括AR 营销、视频会议、导航、教育等。

作为一个额外的优势，Web AR与所有其他趋势很好地结合在一起：它可以成为虚拟世界的接入点，提供虚拟试穿体验，支持实时购物等。

新的 AR 技术对垂直行业的影响

让我们看看它如何影响其中三个。

游戏中的增强现实

自从 Pokemon Go 风靡全球以来，AR 就一直被用于这个利基市场。 从那时起，它就证明了它的优点。

首先，它确实做到了它所承诺的，因为60% 的游戏玩家发现它们很放松，并且几乎相同数量的玩家认为它们是身临其境的。

其次，它为开发者开辟了一个新的维度。 现在他们可以让他们的玩家在不接触设备的情况下控制游戏，只需通过他们的模仿和在空中挥手即可。 最后，AR 允许对现实世界进行无与伦比的社交互动和探索。 从 Pokemon Go 到《行尸走肉：我们的世界》，玩家需要合作获取 AR 资源、对抗敌人等。

医疗保健中的增强现实

通过皮肤和骨骼的实时可见性

  • 外科医生远程操作或指导手术团队
  • 帮助盲人导航和识别物体
  • 培训人们掌握新技术

    • 还有像DermAssist和 EyeDecide 这样的 B2C 应用程序可以帮助普通人快速获取医疗信息并与他们的提供者分享。

    健康行业也使用 AR。 例如， FaceYoga应用程序使用它来吸引人们并通过显示定期锻炼的预期结果来激励他们进行训练。 这些结果是 AR 生成的。

    电子学习中的增强现实

    另一种越来越受欢迎的方法是使用 AR 模拟进行企业培训——这是一种经济高效且安全的新员工入职方式。 它几乎可以用于任何领域，从辅助医疗到铁路运营。

    当然，AR 对儿童具有吸引力。 关键要点

    • 拥有超过 10 亿用户、高参与度和转化率以及大量投资，增强现实在全球范围内呈上升趋势。
    • 现代 AR 的主要趋势包括获得多达 40% 的用户、扩展到工业环境、元界的兴起、Web AR 的增长以及基于视频的社交网络越来越受欢迎。
    • 增强现实影响新的利基市场或在它已经建立的领域中获得更多的重要性：游戏、医疗保健和电子学习。

    如果您想了解更多信息，请继续阅读有关2022 年 7 大未来 AR 趋势的完整博客文章。

    玛丽安维拉尼 - 克里斯·戈德伯—— Feb 12 '21 21 年 2 月 12 日



    原文:

    The post was originally published at Banuba Blog.

    Like many other technologies, AR has shifted from being a sci-fi dream to a gimmick to a routine occurrence. It is nowhere near its apex, however, gaining new users, expanding its scope of use, and synergizing with other cutting-edge tech.

    Here, we will discuss the 7 most notable augmented reality trends for 2022, show 5 interesting stats that underscore its importance, and describe how AR expands into new industries - manufacturing, eLearning, and healthcare.

    Why New AR Tech Matters: 5 Stats

    The importance of augmented reality can be seen in the following numbers.

    • Statista states that more than 1 billion people routinely use AR. Smartphones and tablets remain the most popular tools to interact with it, but workers use smart glasses in plants and factories for this purpose.
    • According to MarketingDive, 15% of retail chains and shops use some form of AR. However, they include world-renowned brands like Nike and IKEA. Moreover, an extra 32% more are either planning to add AR to their shops or are already implementing it.
    • Investment in AR startups also goes up. According to Crunchbase, last year it reached $1.9 billion. Investors expect to see the increased impact of metaverses and expansion of AR in new verticals, as well as more big brands adopting it.
    • There is qualitative growth in augmented reality as well. XRToday reported that the engagement rate of AR grew by 20% and its conversion rate (across all forms) can reach an astronomical 90%.
    • Brands keep investing more in AR advertising. By the beginning of 2023, AR ad revenue will likely be more than $2 billion worldwide.

    7 AR Trends that Will Dominate in 2022

    This is how the world of augmented reality will change this year.

    AR will add 40% more users

    At the beginning of 2022, about a billion people interacted with augmented reality. By the end of the year, this number will increase to 1.4 billion.

    This is due to a combination of factors.

    Firstly, AR becomes more accessible. On the one hand, more people own devices capable of supporting it. On the other, the technology itself is getting more streamlined and optimized. E.g. Banuba Face AR SDK runs even on iPhone 5S and covers 80% of Android devices.

    Web AR, available on anything with a camera and a browser, gains ground as well (more on this later).

    Secondly, enterprise users and government agencies began to appreciate the opportunities that AR offers. Microsoft got a contract with the US military, BMW introduced AR glasses for maintenance specialists, etc.

    Metaverses take the stage

    Metaverse is a shared digital environment that lets people immersively interact with each other similar to how they do it in real life. Neal Stephenson, a sci-fi writer who invented this word, is not impressed with how his idea is being currently implemented.

    Investors, however, see the potential of the technology. Currently, there are several high-profile virtual worlds in development: Decentraland, Meta Platforms, Nvidia Omniverse, and Ali Metaverse to name a few. They want to become hubs for hundreds of thousands of people and the amount of money pouring in shows that they aren’t going away anytime soon.

    For example, JPMorgan made news when it opened the first branch of a bank in a metaverse, calling it a “trillion-dollar opportunity”. And in two years, the metaverse industry will be worth around $80 billion.

    AR live shopping takes over Europe and the USA

    AR-powered live shopping is already big in China, where 10% of individual purchases are done through it. American and European companies are picking up the trend. This includes big names like Meta and smaller startups, and the users eagerly use the new shopping experience.

    On Instagram, for example, 44% percent of users already tried this. And they find it more convenient than live shopping on TikTok.

    By 2023, the live shopping segment will sell $25B worth of goods just in the US market. And in 2026, it would take 20% of the entire eCommerce market.

    There are three main factors influencing the popularity of live shopping.

    It is both convenient and has a person-to-person component of physical shopping Companies that have many social media subscribers can quickly turn them into buyers

    Engaging influencers to promote products is much smoother

    Everybody clones TikTok

    TikTok is the top dog in social media and the Internet overall, getting more traffic than even Google. Other networks try lengthening the videos or adding new features to compete. Still others straight up copy TikTok.

    All the players in the short video market push AR technology to gain an advantage. For example, TikTok and Snapchat made AR effect creation accessible to every user. And while the Chinese company did it only recently, user-created lenses have been viewed more than 3.5 trillion times.

    This trend can be felt all over the world, for example, Chingari, an Indian social network, regularly adds more “Indianized” AR masks and has recently implemented live shopping.

    Brick-and-mortar stores will include AR elements

    Once the COVID-19 restrictions began to disappear, people started returning to physical shops - they just like the process. However, tech-savvy retailers go for the blending of physical and digital (“phygital”) approaches to get the best of both worlds. They develop virtual try-on apps that let people test the products and show where to buy them. They can also install smart mirrors and AR photo booths to make shopping more fun and social media-worthy.

    This approach has the most impact on teenagers and young adults, as a third of Gen-Zers prefer AR-powered stores.

    In most cases, physical store owners start with AR try-on as it is easy to understand and brings tangible advantages - more sales and fewer returns. But there are other options.

    Nike informs people about their supply chains and shows the characteristics of clothing and shoes.

    BMW lets customers change the car’s interior elements in AR to see how it would look, as well as offers virtual test drives.

    Target helps shoppers find specific goods in its locations by placing hundreds of beacons.

    Manufacturers add AR to their toolboxes

    On a more serious note, manufacturing companies have started outfitting their workforce with AR glasses to increase their efficiency. There are four most common use cases for them:

    • Employee onboarding and upskilling. By showing processes step-by-step on actual materials and instruments, companies can help their staff learn new techniques.
    • Item recognition. An AI can find a specific part among dozens of others faster than a person could.
    • Remote work. A specialist can avoid traveling to a remote location by sharing their knowledge through AR overlays.
    • Cell design. Engineers can place AR objects in physical locations to quickly design manufacturing cells.

    There are still issues that prevent wider industrial adoption of AR but the trend is overwhelmingly in its favor - as proven by Dassault, Siemens, Rolls Royce, and other major brands.

    Web AR is skyrocketing

    Web AR is the most accessible implementation of augmented reality for both the users and the businesses, due to low hardware requirements and low maintenance costs.

    The quality and performance of it aren’t as good as with other options, but this is a valid compromise in many cases. Examples include AR marketing, video conferencing, navigation, education, etc.

    As an additional advantage, Web AR combines well with all the other trends: it can be an access point to a metaverse, provide virtual try-on experiences, empower live shopping, etc.

    The impact of new AR tech on verticals

    AR is a technology that can fit many industries. Let’s see how it affects three of them.

    Augmented reality in gaming

    AR has been used in this niche ever since Pokemon Go took the world by storm. Since then it has proven its merit.

    There are three main components to AR gaming’s success.

    Firstly, it really does what it promises, as 60% of gamers find them relaxing and nearly the same number think of them as immersive.

    Secondly, it opens up a new dimension for the developers. Now they can have their players control the game without touching the device, just by their mimic and waving hands in the air. And the AR masks can be either a part of a game or objects to be sold or turned into NFTs.

    Finally, AR allows unparalleled social interaction and exploration of the real world. From Pokemon Go to Walking Dead: Our World, players need to cooperate to obtain AR resources, fight enemies, etc.

    Augmented reality in healthcare

    AR revolutionizes healthcare in a number of ways:

    • Real-time visibility through skin and bone
    • Surgeons operating or guiding surgical teams remotely
    • Helping the blind navigate and recognize objects
    • Train people in new techniques

    There are also B2C apps like DermAssist and EyeDecide that help regular people get medical info quickly and share it with their providers.

    The wellness industry also makes use of AR. For example, the FaceYoga app uses it to attract people and motivate them to train by showing the expected results of regular exercise. These results are AR-generated.

    Augmented reality in eLearning

    AR has been initially used for gamification and has proven to be an effective method for introducing game elements to serious environments.

    Another approach gaining popularity is corporate training with AR simulations - a cost-effective and safe way to onboard new hires. It can be used for almost any niche, from paramedicine to railway operation.

    Of course, AR has traction with children. Apps like Perfect Play, Froggipeia, Skyview, and others help with learning biology, astronomy, and even PE.

    Key takeaways

    • With over 1 billion users, high engagement and conversion rates, and plenty of investment, augmented reality is on the rise globally.
    • The main trends in modern AR include gaining up to 40% more users, spreading to industrial settings, the rise of the metaverses, the growth of Web AR, and video-based social networks gaining more popularity.
    • Augmented reality affects new niches or gains more importance in the ones it has already established itself: gaming, healthcare, and eLearning.

    If you want to learn more, keep reading the full blog post on the Top 7 Future AR Trends in 2022.

    Özgür Adem Işıklı - Feb 14 '21

    MarianVelani - Feb 14 '21

    Răzvan Stătescu - Feb 13 '21

    Chris Godber - Feb 12 '21

item.title: INmatchmaking - 专业的婚介服务 | INmatchmaking - Professional Matchmaking Service
item.description:
该网站已经聚集了来自不同国家的数百万男性和女性观众。 他们在上面注册以进行友好的交流、寻找灵魂伴侣、娱乐和建立家庭。 时尚的设计、易用性和出色的功能吸引了新访客。 该网站可以翻译成世界上不同的语言。 统计数据显示，已有超过 200 万人注册了该服务。 注册仅适用于成人。

要使用该站点，您需要执行一些简单的操作。 填写问卷（姓名、密码、电子邮件）。 确认接受“INmatchmaking”的使用条款和条件。 等待版主确认问卷。 一致。 使用文字和表情符号与任何人私下聊天。 消息和符号的数量是无限的。 使用过滤器搜索。 为了方便搜索合作伙伴，您可以在网站上指定所需的年龄、性别、居住国家、身份。 查看个人资料。 转到页面，查看照片，个人信息。 这个机会将有助于确定合作伙伴的选择。 发送喜欢。 你喜欢这张照片吗？ 在它下面，你可以以点赞的形式留下一个喜欢。 此操作将允许您引起页面所有者的注意。 或许在那之后，愉快的谈话就会开始。 参与社区。 服务上有兴趣小组。 在其中，您可以结识志同道合的人，获取有用的信息并分享有趣的内容。 写日记。 用户可以在电子日记中记录他们的情绪和事件。 送礼物。 某些类型的礼物是免费提供的。 在其中，您可以找到鲜花、糖果、文字赞美等贴纸。 在用户列表中解除调查问卷。 问卷将在服务的顶部，所有用户都会在第一行看到。 这将增加个人资料的出席率，会有更多的消息和礼物。 高级过滤器。 标准清单将包括婚姻状况、生肖、兴趣、眼睛颜色、头发等方面的选择项目。

该网站有使用条款和许可协议。 每个用户都有义务遵守规定，否则他将面临合同规定的封锁和责任。 禁止在网站上散布虚假信息、联系方式和他人照片，禁止在通信中进行威胁、侮辱。 根据“INmatchmaking”的评论，支持服务工作有效，及时响应投诉。

Mbuodile Obiosio - Mbuodile Obiosio - May 12 5月12日



原文:

INmatchmaking - Professional Matchmaking Service Rich functionality, keeping personal diaries, the ability to send likes and gifts, all this is about the INmatchmaking website. The site has gathered a multi-million audience of men and women from different countries. They register on it for friendly communication, finding a soul mate, entertainment and creating a family. Stylish design, ease of use and great features attract new visitors. The site can be translated into different languages of the world. Statistics show that more than 2 million people are registered on the service. Registration is only available to adults.

INmatchmaking - New York Professional Matchmaking Service

To use the site, you need to perform a number of simple actions. Fill out the questionnaire (name, password, email). Confirm acceptance of the terms and conditions of use of "INmatchmaking". Wait for the moderators to confirm the questionnaire. Correspondence. Chat privately with anyone using text and emojis. The number of messages and symbols is unlimited. Search using a filter. For a convenient partner search, you can specify the desired age, gender, country of residence, status on the site. Viewing a profile. Go to the page, see photos, personal information. This opportunity will help determine the choice of a partner. Sending likes. Did you like the photo? Under it, you can leave a liking in the form of a like. This action will allow you to draw the owner of the page to your attention. Perhaps after that, a pleasant conversation will begin. Participation in communities. There are interest groups on the service. In them you can meet like-minded people, get useful information and share interesting content. Keeping a diary. Users can record their emotions and events in an electronic diary. Sending gifts. Some types of gifts are available for free. Among them you can find stickers of flowers, sweets, text compliments and much more. Lifting the questionnaire in the list of users. The questionnaire will be in the top of the service, it will be seen by all users in the first lines. This will increase the profile attendance, there will be more messages and gifts. Advanced filter. The standard list will include items on the choice of marital status, zodiac sign, interests, eye color, hair, and so on.

The site has terms of use and a license agreement. Each user is obliged to comply with the regulations, otherwise he faces blocking and liability established by the contract. It is forbidden to spread false information, contact details and photos of other persons on the site, to threaten, to use insults in correspondence. According to the reviews of "INmatchmaking", the Support Service works effectively, promptly responding to complaints.

Mbuodile Obiosio - May 12

item.title: কম্পেটিটিভপ্রোগ্রামিংনতুনদেরজন্য১০টিঅনলাইনরিভিউরিভিউ | কম্পেটিটিভ প্রোগ্রামিং ও নতুনদের শুরুর জন্য ১০টি অনলাইন জাজের রিভিউ
item.description:
代码力： মোস্টপপুলারজাজ，প্রবলেমএরবেশ。。 代码厨师： এখানেও রেগুলার কন্টেস্ট হয় । 黑客等级： কন্টেস্টরেগুলারহলেওস্পেসিফিকশিখারএবংবেসডশিখারবেশ。。 黑客地球： ওদেরএলগওএরকিছুফিচারজন্যভাল。。 在编码器： নতুনদেরইমপ্লেমেন্টেশনপ্র্যাকটিসজন্যখুবি。。。。。 平台链接：



原文:

কম্পেটিটিভ প্রোগ্রামিং এর শুরুতে আসলে এত্ত এত্ত জাজ চোখে পরে যে অনেক সময় কোনটায় কখন প্রাক্টিস করবো বোঝা যায় না। আবার অনেক সময় প্রশ্ন জাগে যে কোনটা সবচেয়ে ভাল জাজ । তাই মুটামুটি কমন জাজ গুলোর একটা ইন্ট্রো দেওয়া ।

Code Forces : মোস্ট পপুলার অনলাইন জাজ , প্রবলেম এর কুয়ালিটি বেশ ভাল। সবচেয়ে ভাল দিক নিয়মিত কন্টেস্ট হয় এবং প্রচুর পারটিসিপেন্ট থাকে। যারা রেগুলার কন্টেস্ট করতে চায় তাদের জন্য আমার মতে সেরা প্ল্যাটফর্ম । অসুবিধার মধ্য পরে টপিক ভিত্তিক ভাবে গুছানো প্রবলেম পাওয়া, এছাড়া প্রচুর কন্টেস্ট হওয়াতে এই জাজে নতুন নতুন প্রবলেম অ্যাড হতেই থাকে। অনেক ক্ষেত্রেই নতুনরা এত প্রবলেম যাচাই বাছাই করতে পারে না ।

Code Chef : এখানেও রেগুলার কন্টেস্ট হয় । এদের লং কন্টেস্ট নতুনদের শিখার জন্য বেশ ভাল । প্রব্লেম স্টেটমেন্ট বেশ ক্লিয়ার এবং অনেক প্রবলেম স্টেটমেন্ট বাংলাতেও পাওয়া যায় । ওদের প্রবলেম গুলো বেশ সহজ ভাবে লিখা । এছাড়া এদের লারনিং সিরিজে নতুনদের জন্য গুছানো বেশ কিছু প্রবলেম এর কালেকশন আছে ।

Hacker Rank : কন্টেস্ট রেগুলার না হলেও ল্যাঙ্গুয়েজ স্পেসিফিক শিখার জন্য এবং টপিক বেসড শিখার জন্য বেশ ভাল। স্পেশালি সিপিপি এর STL শিখার জন্য খুবি ভাল কিছু মডিউল আছে ।

Hacker Earth : ওদের এলগও ভিজুলাইজেশন এর জন্য কিছু ফিচার শিখার জন্য বেশ ভাল। এছাড়া ওদের কোডমাঙ্ক মডিউলে মুটা মুটি স্টেপ বাই স্টেপ বেশ ভাল একটা গাইড দেওয়া আছে প্রবলেম সল্ভিং এর ।

Light Oj : টপিক বেসড শিখার জন্য খুবি ভাল একটা প্লাটফর্ম । ওদের প্রবলেম এর পরিমাণ কম । মোট হয়তো ৫০০ এরো কম । কিন্তু সব প্রবলেমি খুব ভাল কুয়ালিটির । যারা আইসিপিসি এর জন্য বা এই জাতীয় ফরম্যাট এর জন্য প্রস্তুতি নিচ্ছ তাদের জন্য খুবি ভাল।

At Coder : নতুনদের ইমপ্লেমেন্টেশন প্রবলেম প্র্যাকটিস এর জন্য খুবি ভাল । স্ট্যাটমেন্ট খুবি সহজ ভাবে লিখা । যাদের ইংলিশ একটু দুর্বল বা স্টেটমেন্টে চেয়েছে কি তাই বুঝতেই ধৈর্য শেষ তাদের জন্য খুবি ভাল । এছাড়া মুটা মুটি নিয়মিত কন্টেস্টো হয়।

URI : একেবারে অ আ ক খ এর জন্য চলে। তবে খুব বেশী প্রাকটিস না করাই ভাল । কেউ করলেও ১০০ এর মত সল্ভের পর বাদ দেওয়া ভাল।

UVA : বেশ পুরাতন জাজ । অনেক ভাল সমস্যা আছে এতে , কিন্তু স্টেট্মেন্ট গুলো অনেক বড় , অনেক ক্ষেত্রেই ইনপুট অউটপুট ফরম্যাট ঠিক করতে ভুগতে হয়। তাই প্রধান অনুশীলন এর জন্য না ব্যাবহার করাই ভাল। কিছু বাছাই করা সমস্যার জন্য ব্যাবহার করাই ভাল।

SPOJ : পুরাতন জাজ । অনেকটা আউটডেটেড । ভাল সমস্যা আছে , কিন্তু খুজে বের করতে সময় লাগে।

TIMUS : ম্যাথের অনেক ভাল প্রবলেম আছে । তবে জেনেরাল কেসে ভাল না খুব ।

সবশেষে নতুনদের ক্ষেত্রে, টপিক ধরে শিখার জন্য ভালও LightOj , ল্যাঙ্গুয়েজ প্রফিসিয়েন্সির জন্য Hacker Rank আর রেগুলার কন্টেস্ট এর জন্য Code Forces এবং Code Chef । আমার ব্যাক্তিগত অভিজ্ঞতার ভিত্তিতে মতামত দেয়া। অনেকের প্রেফারেন্স আলাদা হতে পারে।
কারন সব প্ল্যাটফর্মই ভাল ভাল প্রবলেম দেয়া আছে । তবে বেশিরভাগ ক্ষেত্রেই বাছাই করাটা কষ্টের আর সময় সাপেক্ষ। তাই নতুনদের এই চার প্ল্যাটফর্মের মধেই থাকা ভাল ।

item.title: 如何在 React JS/Next JS 中更改滚动导航栏样式 | How to change navbar style on scroll in React JS/Next JS
item.description:
今天我将告诉你改变滚动导航栏样式的最简单方法之一。所以首先。让我们创建一个组件 Header。我正在粘贴 Header 的引导简单代码，只是为了让事情尽可能简单。要使用引导程序的 html css，我们必须首先将其转换为 jsx，您可以通过访问此网站轻松完成。

页眉.jsx 

 < nav className = "navbar navbar-expand-lg navbar-light bg-light" > < a className = "navbar-brand" href = "#" > Navbar </ a > < button className = "navbar-toggler" type = "button" data-toggle = "collapse" data-target = "#navbarSupportedContent" aria-controls = "navbarSupportedContent" aria-expanded = "false" aria-label = "Toggle navigation" > < span className = "navbar-toggler-icon" /> </ button > < div className = "collapse navbar-collapse" id = "navbarSupportedContent" > < ul className = "navbar-nav mr-auto" > < li className = "nav-item active" > < a className = "nav-link" href = "#" > Home < span className = "sr-only" > (current) </ span ></ a > </ li > < li className = "nav-item" > < a className = "nav-link" href = "#" > Link </ a > </ li > < li className = "nav-item dropdown" > < a className = "nav-link dropdown-toggle" href = "#" id = "navbarDropdown" role = "button" data-toggle = "dropdown" aria-haspopup = "true" aria-expanded = "false" > Dropdown </ a > < div className = "dropdown-menu" aria-labelledby = "navbarDropdown" > < a className = "dropdown-item" href = "#" > Action </ a > < a className = "dropdown-item" href = "#" > Another action </ a > < div className = "dropdown-divider" /> < a className = "dropdown-item" href = "#" > Something else here </ a > </ div > </ li > < li className = "nav-item" > < a className = "nav-link disabled" href = "#" > Disabled </ a > </ li > </ ul > < form className = "form-inline my-2 my-lg-0" > < input className = "form-control mr-sm-2" type = "search" placeholder = "Search" aria-label = "Search" /> < button className = "btn btn-outline-success my-2 my-sm-0" type = "submit" > Search </ button > </ form > </ div > </ nav >

它的CSS将是

头文件.css

 .navbar { background-color : #a8dcef ; height : 105px ; width : 100% ; position : fixed ; z-index : 1000 ; border-bottom : 1px solid rgb ( 192 , 192 , 192 ); }

现在我们将使用 useEffect 挂钩

  • 读取滚动位置并将其存储在数据属性中。
  • 监听新的滚动事件。
  • 更新滚动位置。

因此，这是您必须将其粘贴到 Header.jsx 的 useEffect 挂钩中的代码

useEffect (() => { // The debounce function receives our function as a parameter const debounce = ( fn ) => { // This holds the requestAnimationFrame reference, so we can cancel it if we wish let frame ; // The debounce function returns a new function that can receive a variable number of arguments return (... params ) => { // If the frame variable has been defined, clear it now, and queue for next frame if ( frame ) { cancelAnimationFrame ( frame ); } // Queue our function call for the next frame frame = requestAnimationFrame (() => { // Call our function and pass any params we received fn (... params ); }); } }; // Reads out the scroll position and stores it in the data attribute // so we can use it in our stylesheets const storeScroll = () => { document . documentElement . dataset . scroll = window . scrollY ; } // Listen for new scroll events, here we debounce our `storeScroll` function document . addEventListener ( ' scroll ' , debounce ( storeScroll ), { passive : true }); // Update scroll position for first time storeScroll (); })

现在最后一步。为了改变样式，我们可以使用这个 css.In Header.css

 html :not ([ data-scroll = "0" ]) .navbar { position : fixed ; top : 0 ; width : 100% ; /* background-color: #fff; */ opacity : 0.9 ; box-shadow : 0 0 0.2em rgba ( 0 , 0 , 0 , 0.5 ); transition : 0.4s linear ; }

笔记：-

就是这样。现在向下滚动时您会看到变化。

此格式可用于更改滚动格式的任何类

html :not ([ data-scroll = "0" ]) .Here Class name { /* Write the code you want to change */ }

如果您觉得有帮助，请分享！
谢谢。

克里斯蒂安·科扎拉—— 托梅克·波尼亚托维茨 - May 5 5月5日



原文:

Today I am going to tell you one of the simplest ways to change the navbar styling on scroll.So first of all.Lets create a component Header.I am pasting the bootstrap simple code for Header just to keep things as simple as I can.To use the html css of bootstrap we have to first convert it in jsx which you can easily do by going to this website.

htmltojsx Simply paste the code copied from bootstrap navbar and paste it on this website.It will return you with jsx code. Header.jsx

 <nav className="navbar navbar-expand-lg navbar-light bg-light"> <a className="navbar-brand" href="#">Navbar</a> <button className="navbar-toggler" type="button" data-toggle="collapse" data-target="#navbarSupportedContent" aria-controls="navbarSupportedContent" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation"> <span className="navbar-toggler-icon" /> </button> <div className="collapse navbar-collapse" id="navbarSupportedContent"> <ul className="navbar-nav mr-auto"> <li className="nav-item active"> <a className="nav-link" href="#">Home <span className="sr-only">(current)</span></a> </li> <li className="nav-item"> <a className="nav-link" href="#">Link</a> </li> <li className="nav-item dropdown"> <a className="nav-link dropdown-toggle" href="#" id="navbarDropdown" role="button" data-toggle="dropdown" aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded="false"> Dropdown </a> <div className="dropdown-menu" aria-labelledby="navbarDropdown"> <a className="dropdown-item" href="#">Action</a> <a className="dropdown-item" href="#">Another action</a> <div className="dropdown-divider" /> <a className="dropdown-item" href="#">Something else here</a> </div> </li> <li className="nav-item"> <a className="nav-link disabled" href="#">Disabled</a> </li> </ul> <form className="form-inline my-2 my-lg-0"> <input className="form-control mr-sm-2" type="search" placeholder="Search" aria-label="Search" /> <button className="btn btn-outline-success my-2 my-sm-0" type="submit">Search</button> </form> </div> </nav>

and its css will be

Header.css

.navbar { background-color: #a8dcef; height: 105px; width: 100%; position: fixed; z-index: 1000; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(192, 192, 192);
}

Now we will use useEffect hook to

  • Read the scroll position and store it in the data attribute.
  • Listen for new scroll events.
  • Update scroll position.

So this is the code you have to paste it in useEffect hook in Header.jsx

useEffect(() => { // The debounce function receives our function as a parameter const debounce = (fn) => { // This holds the requestAnimationFrame reference, so we can cancel it if we wish let frame; // The debounce function returns a new function that can receive a variable number of arguments return (...params) => { // If the frame variable has been defined, clear it now, and queue for next frame if (frame) { cancelAnimationFrame(frame); } // Queue our function call for the next frame frame = requestAnimationFrame(() => { // Call our function and pass any params we received fn(...params); }); } }; // Reads out the scroll position and stores it in the data attribute // so we can use it in our stylesheets const storeScroll = () => { document.documentElement.dataset.scroll = window.scrollY; } // Listen for new scroll events, here we debounce our `storeScroll` function document.addEventListener('scroll', debounce(storeScroll), { passive: true }); // Update scroll position for first time storeScroll(); })

Now Last Step.For changing the styling we can use this css.In Header.css

html:not([data-scroll="0"]) .navbar { position: fixed; top: 0; width: 100%; /* background-color: #fff; */ opacity: 0.9; box-shadow: 0 0 0.2em rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5); transition: 0.4s linear;
}

Note:-

And thats it.Now you will see a change when you scroll down.

This format can be used to change any class on scroll the format is

html:not([data-scroll="0"]) .Here Class name { /* Write the code you want to change */
}

Share it if you found it helpful!
Thanks.

Intesar Shannan Mohammed - May 9

Christian Kozalla - May 11

Tomek Poniatowicz - May 5
